title: EU DORA Operational Resilience Update
content:
MEMORANDUM

TO: All Advisory Staff (Tier 2 and Above)
FROM: Compliance Department
DATE: March 15, 2025
RE: EU DORA Operational Resilience Framework - Implementation Update

As we approach the full implementation of the Digital Operational Resilience Act (EU DORA Regulation 2022/2554), effective January 17, 2025, Meridian Wealth Partners has completed Phase II of our operational resilience enhancement program. This memo provides critical updates on our compliance posture and required actions for client-facing advisory teams.

**ICT Risk Management Framework Updates**

Our newly implemented ICT risk management framework now incorporates enhanced monitoring capabilities for all critical third-party service providers. Following DORA Article 5 requirements, we have established continuous monitoring of our portfolio management systems, with automated alerts triggering when system availability drops below 99.5%. Our primary custody partner, Northern Trust, has confirmed DORA compliance for all client assets under management, representing €847 million across 1,247 client portfolios as of February 2025.

**Incident Reporting Mechanisms**

Effective immediately, all advisors must report any ICT-related incidents impacting client services within 2 hours to our dedicated DORA hotline (extension 4477). Major incidents, defined as those affecting more than €10 million in assets under management or disrupting services for over 30 minutes, require immediate escalation to supervising authorities within 24 hours per Article 19 requirements.

**Third-Party Provider Risk Assessment**

Our critical ICT third-party providers have undergone comprehensive DORA compliance assessments. Bloomberg Terminal services, affecting 89% of our advisory operations, achieved full compliance certification in January 2025. Our backup trading platform, Charles River IMS, completed required penetration testing with a 98.7% security score. Two minor providers servicing our Luxembourg office showed deficiencies and will be replaced by Q2 2025.

**Digital Operational Resilience Testing**

Meridian has scheduled mandatory resilience testing for April 2025, simulating a complete primary system failure affecting €200 million in client portfolios. All Tier 2+ advisors must participate in the 4-hour simulation exercise. Recovery time objectives have been established at 45 minutes for critical trading functions and 2 hours for complete portfolio access restoration.

**ICT-Related Incident Classification**

New classification thresholds align with DORA Article 18 specifications:
- Level 1: Incidents affecting individual client accounts (<€1 million AUM)
- Level 2: Incidents impacting multiple portfolios (€1-50 million AUM)
- Level 3: Major incidents requiring regulatory notification (>€50 million AUM)

**Client Communication Protocols**

Advisors must inform affected clients within 4 business hours of any Level 2 or Level 3 incidents. Standard communication templates are available on our compliance portal. For high-net-worth clients (>€5 million AUM), direct phone contact is mandatory within 2 hours.

**Training Requirements**

All advisory staff must complete DORA operational resilience training by March 31, 2025. The 90-minute module covers incident response procedures, client communication protocols, and business continuity activation. Completion is tracked through our LMS system and required for Q1 performance evaluations.

**Regulatory Oversight**

Our Luxembourg financial supervisory authority (CSSF) has scheduled a DORA compliance inspection for May 2025. All client files, incident logs, and third-party contracts must be readily accessible for review. Advisory teams should ensure proper documentation of all technology-related client interactions since January 2025.

For questions regarding DORA compliance requirements or specific client situations, contact the Compliance Department at compliance@meridianwealth.eu or extension 4401.

Regards,
Sarah Mitchell
Head of Regulatory Compliance
Meridian Wealth Partners
